commits: 028295a: docs(12-DX): external user docs — install + quickstart + reference + concepts/compatibility
api_ref_tooling_rationale: mkdocstrings + mkdocstrings-python ARE installed (0.30.1 / 2.0.3) and would work with the basic mkdocs theme, but Python source docstrings are sparse (29 triple-quoted strings across 6 modules; most functions have no docstring beyond the module-level prose). Adding docstrings to drive mkdocstrings = scope creep + drift risk against locked interface specs. Hand-written ref pages cite dev/interfaces/*.md as authoritative, keep LoC bounded, and stay aligned with the locked specs by construction. TS is hand-written per spec (avoids npm typedoc dep). Rust ref defers to docs.rs post-publish + GitHub source link pre-GA per spec.
quickstart_smoke_equivalence: matches — quickstart's full Python program (docs/getting-started/quickstart.md § Full Python program) is byte-for-byte the body of scripts/release/smoke/smoke-pypi-wheel.sh with print(engine.counters()) added between search() and close(). Same 5 operations in same order: Engine.open → write([]) → search(query) → close() → print('ok'). No divergence; AC-056 gate text and client experience aligned.
deferral_disclosures_visible_from: compatibility/index.md (performance gates AC-012/013/019/020; no-0.5.x-shim; open-report deferral; logical-id deferral)
reference/errors.md (recovery hint codes + corruption recovery worked example)
reference/cli.md (logical-id verb deferral + client workaround via --excise-source)
concepts/index.md (logical-id deferral mentioned in recovery surface section)
loc_budget_note: Spec budget was ~500-800 LoC of markdown. Actual additions land at ~1217 net new lines. Overshoot driven by 5 reference pages (python-api 167 + typescript-api 173 + cli 95 + errors 102 + config 81 = 618 LoC) which carry the locked-surface field-by-field documentation that clients need to actually use the SDKs. Each reference page is bounded (≤173 LoC) and cites the authoritative spec rather than duplicating it. Net-negative-LoC bias was applied to guides/operations (left as post-GA stubs) and to concepts/compatibility (60-100 LoC tight overview, not deep treatment). If orchestrator/reviewer wants the LoC under 800, candidates to trim are the worked-example block in errors.md and the per-step prose in quickstart.md, but neither is recommended — both serve concrete client utility.

blockers_encountered.description: scripts/agent-verify.sh hits AC-036/AC-037 strace BLOCKER on bare host (no sudo available to install strace). Documented in spec § 'Known flakes' — bootstrap installs strace; this is the 12-S baseline behavior on hosts without strace pre-installed. UNRELATED to docs slice content.
blockers_encountered.impact: agent-verify reports '0 violation(s), 2 blocker(s)'. lint/typecheck/test/FTS5-injection/ast-scan-{rust,python,ts}/removal-detect all PASS.
blockers_encountered.resolution: documented; orchestrator/reviewer to confirm strace blocker is acceptable for a docs-only slice. No code or docs change required.

blockers_NOT_encountered: mkdocstrings vs basic-theme compat — not exercised (went hand-written per spec rationale).
Python source docstring sparseness — confirmed sparse but moot once hand-written path chosen.
quickstart-vs-smoke divergence — verified equivalent; documented in quickstart_smoke_equivalence above.
pre-GA install paths breaking on dev host — not verified live (docs-only slice; install pages cite the documented commands).
agent_verify_result: lint/typecheck/test/FTS5-injection/ast-scan(rust,python,ts)/removal-detect PASS; AC-036/AC-037 strace BLOCKER (host-config, 12-S baseline, no sudo for apt install strace). Tail: 'agent-security: 0 violation(s), 2 blocker(s)'.
next_step_for_orchestrator: promote to 0.6.0-rewrite; respawn codex reviewer for verdict
